author: jsign
content:
Do you mean to `ere-guests`? Since `zkevm-benchmark-workload` pulls ELFs from `ere-guests`.
Reg v0.18.0, <@466102668138512394>  was working on this in `ere` today so, I would expect this to land in `ere-guests` soon.

author: jsign
content:
The way we are thinking of new guest program integration is by pulling executable-ELFs from EL guest repos (e.g. releases). Mainly because we want to avoid `ere-guests` to fully compile the ELF given there are so many different languages.

Until the zkVM standards are more broadly supported by everybody, pulling final ELFs should be okay. But the idea is that guest repos publish a relocatable-ELF, and in ere-guests we can do the final linking with each zkVM precompile to generate the final ELF per zkVM.

So guest programs can only compile a single relocatable-ELF and don't have to deal with multiple zkVMs, and ere-guests can do the final linking and generate multiple final ELFs per zkVM that supports the standard.

(As a middle ground for now should be fine for guest repos to publish final-ELFs (e.g. targeting Zisk or simialr).. mostly sharing the idea here).

author: jsign
content: BTW, this is a rather new take on how to integrate non-Rust guest programs (and eventually Rust ones too). So we still have to do some work in ere-guests to support this flow! Working on this soon

author: maximmenshikov_
content: That's great, looking forward to ere-guests update.

author: maximmenshikov_
content:
<@774033563732541451> we indeed have Nethermind integrated now, just probably not the iconic way that is expected.

I would rather vote against relocatable ELFs for now, though I didn't fully investigate if we can work with relocatable ones.

author: jsign
content: Cool, yeah I think for a while we can start with final ELFs that you can publish... and after some things settle in zkVM standards support we can dig deeper into a more modular pipeline if possible. Let's go in steps to make integration simpler
